title: Bridge Essay: A corollary discharge circuit in human speech.
concept: Voice-hearing as spectrum phenomenon — auditory verbal hallucinations, agency detection, inner speech.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.
**Phase context:** Voice-hearing as spectrum phenomenon — auditory verbal hallucinations, agency detection, inner speech.

**A corollary discharge circuit in human speech.** — Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2024). DOI: 10.1073/pnas.2404121121. PMID: 39625978. When we vocalize, our brain distinguishes self-generated sounds from external ones. A corollary discharge signal supports this function in animals; however, in humans, its exact origin and temporal dynamics remain unknown. We report electrocorticographic recordings in neurosurgical patients and a connectivity analysis framework based on Granger causality that reveals major neural communications. We find a reproducible source for corollary discharge across multiple speech production paradigms loc

notes: Auto-acquired from PubMed/MEDLINE on 2026-07-12. Phase 13 (Daimon). Evaluation: Good (score=2.9)
